result = (
    result.faces(">X").workplane()
    .circle(hole_r).cutBlind(depth_x)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Create main central cylinder along X axis
cyl_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(20, 6.5)

# Create central rectangular pad (top and bottom bosses)
pad = cq.Workplane("XY").box(12, 10, 15)

# Create Y-axis port with O-ring groove
y_port = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
          .circle(6.5)
          .extrude(11)
          .faces(">Y")
          .workplane()
          .circle(4.5)
          .extrude(2)
          .faces(">Y")
          .workplane()
          .circle(6.5)
          .extrude(3))

# Create hex nuts on both ends of the X axis
hex_r = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=10).polygon(6, 22).extrude(10)
hex_l = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-10).polygon(6, 22).extrude(-10)

# Combine all solid bodies
result = (cyl_x
          .union(pad, clean=True)
          .union(y_port, clean=True)
          .union(hex_r, clean=True)
          .union(hex_l, clean=True))

# Add through-hole for the main X axis
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().hole(8)

# Add intersecting blind hole for the Y port
# Depth is chosen to fully intersect the X-axis hole without punching through the back wall
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().hole(8, depth=18)

# Add edge chamfers to represent machined outer features and inner hole chamfers
result = result.edges(">X").chamfer(0.8)
result = result.edges("<X").chamfer(0.8)
result = result.edges(">Y").chamfer(0.8)

import cadquery as cq

# Construct the central forged T-body
# Base rim profile
rim_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-10, -6).lineTo(10, -6).lineTo(10, 6).lineTo(6, 6)
    .lineTo(6, 10).lineTo(-6, 10).lineTo(-6, 6).lineTo(-10, 6).close()
)

# Core profile (offset inwards by 1.5mm to create the raised rim / recessed flat look)
core_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-8.5, -4.5).lineTo(8.5, -4.5).lineTo(8.5, 4.5).lineTo(4.5, 4.5)
    .lineTo(4.5, 8.5).lineTo(-4.5, 8.5).lineTo(-4.5, 4.5).lineTo(-8.5, 4.5).close()
)

# Extrude the main body and apply fillets to the vertical edges
body = rim_profile.extrude(12).translate((0, 0, -6)).edges("|Z").fillet(1.5)

# Create cutting tools to form the shallow recesses on the top and bottom faces
cut_top = core_profile.extrude(2).translate((0, 0, 5)).edges("|Z").fillet(0.5)
cut_bottom = core_profile.extrude(2).translate((0, 0, -7)).edges("|Z").fillet(0.5)

# Apply the cuts to get the final forged body shape
body = body.cut(cut_top).cut(cut_bottom)

# Construct the right run branch (with a compression nut)
# Overlaps slightly into the body for robust union
right_gap = cq.Workplane("YZ", origin=(9.5, 0, 0)).circle(4.5).extrude(2.6) 
nut = cq.Workplane("YZ", origin=(12, 0, 0)).polygon(6, 16).extrude(10)
nut = nut.faces(">X").edges().chamfer(1.2)
nut = nut.faces("<X").edges().chamfer(1.2)
tube = cq.Workplane("YZ", origin=(21.5, 0, 0)).circle(3.5).extrude(4.5)

right_run = right_gap.union(nut).union(tube)

# The left run is symmetric, so we just mirror the right one
left_run = right_run.mirror("YZ")

# Construct the bare branch on the Y-axis (male threaded end)
y_thread = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(0, 9.5, 0)).circle(4.5).extrude(6.5)
y_cone = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(0, 15.5, 0)).circle(3.5).extrude(3.5)
y_cone = y_cone.faces(">Y").edges().chamfer(0.6)
y_branch = y_thread.union(y_cone)

# Combine all solid components
result = body.union(right_run).union(left_run).union(y_branch)

# Hollow out the fitting with proper intersecting fluid channels
# Drill the main run passage completely through
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().hole(4.5)
# Drill the branch passage down to intersect the main channel
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().hole(4.5, depth=18)

import cadquery as cq

# Define base workplanes to explicitly align with the main axes
# wp_run points along +X axis
wp_run = cq.Workplane("YZ") 
# wp_branch points along +Y axis
wp_branch = cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(-90, 0, 0)) 

# 1. Central Body
# Create the main T-junction structure
body_run = wp_run.workplane(offset=-11).circle(7.0).extrude(22.0)
body_branch = wp_branch.circle(7.0).extrude(11.0)
body = body_run.union(body_branch)

# Cut the flat wrench surfaces on the top and bottom (Z axis)
box_top = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=5.5).rect(50, 50).extrude(10.0)
box_bottom = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-5.5).rect(50, 50).extrude(-10.0)
body = body.cut(box_top).cut(box_bottom)

# 2. Run Ports (+X and -X sides)
# Positive X side collar and release ring
c1 = wp_run.workplane(offset=11).circle(6.0).extrude(4.5).faces(">X").chamfer(0.8)
r1 = wp_run.workplane(offset=15.5).circle(4.5).extrude(3.0).faces(">X").chamfer(0.5)

# Negative X side collar and release ring
c2 = wp_run.workplane(offset=-11).circle(6.0).extrude(-4.5).faces("<X").chamfer(0.8)
r2 = wp_run.workplane(offset=-15.5).circle(4.5).extrude(-3.0).faces("<X").chamfer(0.5)

# 3. Branch Port (+Y side)
# Hexagonal nut section with filleted edges
hex_nut = wp_branch.workplane(offset=11).polygon(6, 16.0).extrude(4.5).edges("|Y").fillet(0.5)

# O-ring retainer washer, groove, and threaded fitting tip
washer = wp_branch.workplane(offset=15.5).circle(7.2).extrude(1.2)
groove = wp_branch.workplane(offset=16.7).circle(5.2).extrude(1.8)
thread = wp_branch.workplane(offset=18.5).circle(6.5).extrude(6.0).faces(">Y").chamfer(1.2)

# Black rubber O-ring represented as a torus
oring = cq.Workplane().add(cq.Solid.makeTorus(6.1, 0.9, cq.Vector(0, 17.6, 0), cq.Vector(0, 1, 0)))

# Union all solid features together
result = (body
          .union(c1).union(r1)
          .union(c2).union(r2)
          .union(hex_nut).union(washer).union(groove).union(thread).union(oring))

# 4. Inner Hollow Passages
# Main narrow passages for fluid flow
hole_run_main = wp_run.workplane(offset=-25).circle(2.5).extrude(50.0)
hole_branch_main = wp_branch.workplane(offset=-10).circle(2.5).extrude(40.0)

# Stepped outer holes for push-to-connect tubing insertion
hole_run_pos = wp_run.workplane(offset=18.5).circle(3.2).extrude(-8.0)
hole_run_neg = wp_run.workplane(offset=-18.5).circle(3.2).extrude(8.0)
hole_branch_end = wp_branch.workplane(offset=24.5).circle(3.5).extrude(-8.0)

# Cut the internal channels to finalize the model
result = (result
          .cut(hole_run_main)
          .cut(hole_run_pos)
          .cut(hole_run_neg)
          .cut(hole_branch_main)
          .cut(hole_branch_end))

import cadquery as cq

# --- 1. T Body Profile ---
# Create the central forged body using a profile extruded symmetrically
pts = [
    (8.5, -6.0),
    (8.5, 6.0),
    (6.0, 6.0),
    (6.0, 8.5),
    (-6.0, 8.5),
    (-6.0, 6.0),
    (-8.5, 6.0),
    (-8.5, -6.0)
]
t_profile = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(pts).close().extrude(6.0, both=True)
# Fillet the edges parallel to Y to give it a forged look
t_profile = t_profile.edges("|Y").fillet(1.5)

# Add cylindrical bosses for the threaded connections
# Slight overlaps (e.g. 8.4 instead of 8.5) ensure robust boolean unions
boss_x1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=8.4).circle(6.0).extrude(2.6)
boss_x2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-11.0).circle(6.0).extrude(2.6)
boss_z = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=8.4).circle(6.0).extrude(2.6)

body = t_profile.union(boss_x1).union(boss_x2).union(boss_z)

# --- 2. Right Nut (X+) ---
# Base hex stock
hex_stock_r = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=10.0).polygon(6, 16.5).extrude(10.0)
# Revolve a profile to create the rear chamfer and front conical taper
nut_profile_r = (cq.Workplane("XY")
                 .polyline([(10.0, 0.0), (10.0, 7.5), (11.0, 8.5), (17.0, 8.5), (20.0, 6.5), (20.0, 0.0)])
                 .close()
                 .revolve(360, (0,0,0), (1,0,0)))
right_nut = hex_stock_r.intersect(nut_profile_r)
# Add stepped cylinder and inserted tube
right_nut = right_nut.union(cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=19.9).circle(6.5).extrude(2.1))
right_nut = right_nut.union(cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=21.9).circle(3.5).extrude(4.6))

# --- 3. Left Nut (X-) ---
left_nut = right_nut.mirror("YZ")

# --- 4. Branch (Z+) ---
# Base hex stock for the branch
branch_hex_stock = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=10.0).polygon(6, 16.5).extrude(4.0)
# Profile to give the branch hex a small chamfer at the bottom
branch_hex_profile = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
                      .polyline([(0.0, 10.0), (7.5, 10.0), (8.5, 11.0), (8.5, 14.0), (0.0, 14.0)])
                      .close()
                      .revolve(360, (0,0,0), (0,1,0)))
branch_hex = branch_hex_stock.intersect(branch_hex_profile)

# Revolve the rest of the branch (washer, groove, threads, chamfer)
branch_rev_pts = [
    (0.0, 13.9),
    (7.5, 13.9),
    (7.5, 15.0),
    (5.25, 15.0),
    (5.25, 16.5),
    (6.0, 16.5),
    (6.0, 21.0),
    (5.0, 22.5),
    (0.0, 22.5)
]
branch_rev = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(branch_rev_pts).close().revolve(360, (0,0,0), (0,1,0))

# O-ring embedded in the groove
oring = cq.Workplane("XZ").moveTo(6.05, 15.75).circle(0.7).revolve(360, (0,0,0), (0,1,0))

# --- 5. Combine and Cut Holes ---
# Combine all solid components
result = (body
          .union(right_nut)
          .union(left_nut)
          .union(branch_hex)
          .union(branch_rev)
          .union(oring))

# Cut the continuous internal flow path
hole_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").circle(2.2).extrude(60.0, both=True)
hole_z = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(2.2).extrude(30.0)
holes = hole_x.union(hole_z)

result = result.cut(holes)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters inferred from the images
d_main = 12.7           # Main cylinder diameter
l_tail = 22.0           # Length of the tail section before the collar
d_collar = 15.875       # Collar diameter
l_collar = 5.0          # Collar length

l_branch = 14.0         # Length of branch cylinders before the nut
l_nut = 12.0            # Nut length
d_nut_hex = 25.4        # Hex nut diameter (across corners)

box_l = 8.0             # Central tab length (along X)
box_w = 8.0             # Central tab width (along Y)
box_h = 16.0            # Central tab total height (along Z)

d_hole = 7.0            # Internal through-hole diameter

# 1. Central Junction Base
# A sphere at the origin smoothly blends the intersecting cylinders
result = cq.Workplane("XY").sphere(d_main / 2.0)

# Add the central rectangular tabs
tab_box = cq.Workplane("XY").box(box_l, box_w, box_h)
result = result.union(tab_box)

# 2. Tail Section (along +X axis)
tail = cq.Workplane("YZ").circle(d_main / 2.0).extrude(l_tail)
collar = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=l_tail).circle(d_collar / 2.0).extrude(l_collar)
result = result.union(tail).union(collar)

# 3. Nut Prototype Creation
# Create a hex prism base
nut = cq.Workplane("XY").polygon(6, d_nut_hex).extrude(l_nut)

# Create a conical cutter to produce the characteristic circular chamfer on the nut's outer face
R_corner = d_nut_hex / 2.0
R_flat = R_corner * math.cos(math.pi / 6.0)
c_r = R_flat * 0.95  # Slightly smaller than flat radius to ensure a full circular cut

p1 = (c_r, l_nut)
p2 = (R_corner + 2.0, l_nut)
p3 = (R_corner + 2.0, l_nut - (R_corner + 2.0 - c_r))

cutter = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
          .moveTo(*p1).lineTo(*p2).lineTo(*p3).close()
          .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)))
nut = nut.cut(cutter)

# 4. Branch Sections
# Branch 1 (Rotated 135 degrees from +X axis)
b1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").transformed(rotate=(0, 0, 135)).circle(d_main / 2.0).extrude(l_branch)
n1 = nut.translate((0, 0, l_branch)).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), 90).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 135)
result = result.union(b1).union(n1)

# Branch 2 (Rotated 225 / -135 degrees from +X axis)
b2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").transformed(rotate=(0, 0, 225)).circle(d_main / 2.0).extrude(l_branch)
n2 = nut.translate((0, 0, l_branch)).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), 90).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 225)
result = result.union(b2).union(n2)

# 5. Through Holes
# Negative cylinders extended through the center origin to ensure clean intersections
h_tail = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-10.0).circle(d_hole / 2.0).extrude(l_tail + l_collar + 20.0)
h_b1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").transformed(rotate=(0, 0, 135)).workplane(offset=-10.0).circle(d_hole / 2.0).extrude(l_branch + l_nut + 20.0)
h_b2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").transformed(rotate=(0, 0, 225)).workplane(offset=-10.0).circle(d_hole / 2.0).extrude(l_branch + l_nut + 20.0)

result = result.cut(h_tail).cut(h_b1).cut(h_b2)

# 6. Final Detailing
# Chamfer the edges of the tail's flat end face
result = result.faces(">X").edges().chamfer(0.5)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Base dimensions inferred from the multi-view image proportions
D_body = 14.0
R = D_body / 2.0
D_bore = 8.0

# 1. Main Run Cylinder (X-axis)

run_cyl = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-14.0).circle(R).extrude(26.0)

# 2. Branch Cylinder (Y-axis)
# Represents the cylindrical body of the 90-degree branch
branch_cyl = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=0.0).circle(R).extrude(12.0)

# 3. Forged Central Block
# Creates the flat-sided forged T-junction with rounded edges.
# Centered to perfectly blend with the cylinders.
block = (cq.Workplane("XY")
         .center(0, 5.0)
         .box(16.0, 10.0, 14.0)
         .edges()
         .fillet(1.5))

# 4. Port 1 Features (Left Run - Compression Nut)
nut1_hex = (cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-20.0)
            .polygon(6, 19.0 * 2.0 / math.sqrt(3))
            .extrude(6.0)
            .faces("<X").edges().chamfer(1.0))

nut1_collar = (cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-22.0)
               .circle(12.0 / 2.0)
               .extrude(2.0)
               .faces("<X").edges().chamfer(0.5))

# 5. Port 3 Features (Branch - Compression Nut)
nut3_hex = (cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=12.0)
            .polygon(6, 19.0 * 2.0 / math.sqrt(3))
            .extrude(6.0)
            .faces(">Y").edges().chamfer(1.0))

nut3_collar = (cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=18.0)
               .circle(12.0 / 2.0)
               .extrude(2.0)
               .faces(">Y").edges().chamfer(0.5))

# 6. Port 2 Features (Right Run - Stepped Male End)
hex2 = (cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=12.0)
        .polygon(6, 17.0 * 2.0 / math.sqrt(3))
        .extrude(6.0)
        .faces(">X").edges().chamfer(1.0))

male1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=18.0).circle(14.0 / 2.0).extrude(2.0)
male2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=20.0).circle(11.5 / 2.0).extrude(2.0) # Groove
male3 = (cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=22.0)
         .circle(14.0 / 2.0)
         .extrude(4.0)
         .faces(">X").edges().chamfer(1.0))

# 7. Combine all solid external features
result = (run_cyl
          .union(branch_cyl)
          .union(block)
          .union(nut1_hex)
          .union(nut1_collar)
          .union(nut3_hex)
          .union(nut3_collar)
          .union(hex2)
          .union(male1)
          .union(male2)
          .union(male3))

# 8. Cut internal flow paths (bores)
bore_run = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-25.0).circle(D_bore / 2.0).extrude(55.0)
bore_branch = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-5.0).circle(D_bore / 2.0).extrude(30.0)

result = result.cut(bore_run).cut(bore_branch)

import cadquery as cq

# Base dimensions inferred from the proportions in the images
W = 12.5          # Width of the square central body
L = 16.0          # Distance from center to the square end faces
nut_D = 16.0      # Circumscribed diameter of the hexagonal nut
nut_L = 8.0       # Length of the hexagonal nut
pipe_OR = 3.175   # Outer radius of the pipe extension
pipe_L = 8.0      # Length of the pipe extension
hole_IR = 2.0     # Radius of the inner hollow channel

# Create the central T-shaped block
body = cq.Workplane("XY").box(L * 2.0, W, W)
branch = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0.0, L / 2.0).box(W, L, W)
body = body.union(branch)

# Helper function to create an arm (hex nut + chamfer + pipe)
def make_arm(rot_angle):
    # Hexagonal base with specific rotation for flat alignment
    arm = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .transformed(rotate=(0, 0, rot_angle))
        .polygon(6, nut_D)
        .extrude(nut_L)
    )
    
    # Revolve cut to create the conical chamfer on the nut
    cut_prof = (
        cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .moveTo(5.0, nut_L)
        .lineTo(10.0, nut_L)
        .lineTo(10.0, nut_L - 5.0)
        .close()
    )
    arm = arm.cut(cut_prof.revolve(360.0, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)))
    
    # Add the straight pipe section
    arm = arm.faces(">Z").workplane().circle(pipe_OR).extrude(pipe_L)
    
    return arm.val()

# Create arms with flat alignments matched to the references
# arm_X flat normal must point correctly after 90 deg Y-axis rotation
arm_X = make_arm(30.0) 
# arm_Y flat normal must point correctly after -90 deg X-axis rotation
arm_Y = make_arm(0.0)  

# Setup transformations to snap arms perfectly onto the central block faces
loc1 = cq.Location(cq.Vector(L, 0, 0), cq.Vector(0, 1, 0), 90.0)
loc2 = cq.Location(cq.Vector(-L, 0, 0), cq.Vector(0, 1, 0), -90.0)
loc3 = cq.Location(cq.Vector(0, L, 0), cq.Vector(1, 0, 0), -90.0)

# Merge everything into a single solid 
result = (
    body.union(cq.Workplane().add(arm_X.located(loc1)))
        .union(cq.Workplane().add(arm_X.located(loc2)))
        .union(cq.Workplane().add(arm_Y.located(loc3)))
)

# Drill the hollow fluid pathways
# X-axis channel cuts cleanly through the full length of the straight section
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().circle(hole_IR).cutBlind(80.0)

# Y-axis channel cuts down just past the origin to intersect the main flow path
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(hole_IR).cutBlind(35.0)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Proportional dimensions inferred from the multi-view image
body_w = 12.0
main_len = 16.0
branch_len = 8.0

cyl_d = 10.0
cyl_l = 2.0

nut_w = 14.0
nut_l = 8.0
chamfer_val = 1.0

tube_d = 6.0
tube_l = 8.0

inner_d = 4.0

# 1. Central T-body
main_body = cq.Workplane("XY").box(main_len, body_w, body_w)
branch_body = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, branch_len / 2.0).box(body_w, branch_len, body_w)
body = main_body.union(branch_body)

# 2. Reusable branch generation
def make_branch_solid():
    # Calculate circumscribed diameter for the hex nut to achieve the desired width across flats
    nut_D = nut_w / math.cos(math.radians(30))
    
    # Base cylinder (buries 1mm backwards into the main body to ensure robust boolean union)
    b = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1.0).circle(cyl_d / 2.0).extrude(cyl_l + 1.0)
    
    # Hex nut with chamfers on both the front and back faces
    nut = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=cyl_l).polygon(6, nut_D).extrude(nut_l)
    nut = nut.faces(">Z").edges().chamfer(chamfer_val)
    nut = nut.faces("<Z").edges().chamfer(chamfer_val)
    
    b = b.union(nut)
    
    # Extending tube
    tube = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=cyl_l + nut_l).circle(tube_d / 2.0).extrude(tube_l)
    b = b.union(tube)
    
    return b

# 3. Create and position the three branches
br = make_branch_solid()

right_br = br.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), 90).translate((main_len / 2.0, 0, 0))
left_br  = br.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), -90).translate((-main_len / 2.0, 0, 0))
top_br   = br.rotate((0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0), -90).translate((0, branch_len, 0))

# 4. Assemble the complete solid fitting
result = body.union(right_br).union(left_br).union(top_br)

# 5. Drill the internal fluid passages
total_len_X = main_len / 2.0 + cyl_l + nut_l + tube_l
total_len_Y = branch_len + cyl_l + nut_l + tube_l

# Main run passage (drills entirely through from right to left)
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().hole(inner_d, depth=total_len_X * 2.0)

# Branch passage (drills down and safely intersects the main passage)
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().hole(inner_d, depth=total_len_Y + 4.0)

import cadquery as cq

# Geometric parameters inferred from the multi-view image
D_cyl = 12.0
R_cyl = D_cyl / 2.0
L_cyl_half = 13.0

W_hex = 18.0
D_hex = W_hex / 0.866025  # Circumscribed diameter for 6-sided polygon
L_hex = 10.0

D_tube_out = 8.0
R_tube_out = D_tube_out / 2.0
L_tube = 10.0

D_t_boss = 10.0
R_t_boss = D_t_boss / 2.0
L_t_boss = 12.0  # From center

W_pad = 10.0
W_pad_top = 6.0
H_pad_base_center = 5.0
H_pad_base_height = 4.0
H_pad_top_center = 7.5
H_pad_top_height = 1.0

D_hole = 5.0
R_hole = D_hole / 2.0

# 1. Create central cylindrical body along X-axis
body = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(L_cyl_half * 2.0, R_cyl)

# 2. Add perpendicular T-branch boss along +Y-axis
t_boss = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=L_t_boss / 2.0).cylinder(L_t_boss, R_t_boss)
body = body.union(t_boss)

# 3. Add stepped square wrench pad on top (+Z-axis)
pad_base = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=H_pad_base_center).box(W_pad, W_pad, H_pad_base_height)
pad_top = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=H_pad_top_center).box(W_pad_top, W_pad_top, H_pad_top_height)
body = body.union(pad_base).union(pad_top)

# 4. Add +X Hex Nut with conical chamfer
nut1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=L_cyl_half).polygon(6, D_hex).extrude(L_hex)
cone1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=L_cyl_half + L_hex).circle(R_tube_out + 2.0).workplane(offset=-L_hex).circle(W_hex).loft()
nut1 = nut1.intersect(cone1)
body = body.union(nut1)

# 5. Add -X Hex Nut with conical chamfer
nut2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-(L_cyl_half + L_hex)).polygon(6, D_hex).extrude(L_hex)
cone2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-(L_cyl_half + L_hex)).circle(R_tube_out + 2.0).workplane(offset=L_hex).circle(W_hex).loft()
nut2 = nut2.intersect(cone2)
body = body.union(nut2)

# 6. Add protruding tubes
tube1 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=L_cyl_half + L_hex).circle(R_tube_out).extrude(L_tube)
tube2 = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-(L_cyl_half + L_hex + L_tube)).circle(R_tube_out).extrude(L_tube)
tube3 = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=L_t_boss).circle(R_tube_out).extrude(L_tube)
body = body.union(tube1).union(tube2).union(tube3)

# 7. Cut internal fluid passages
# Main bore through the X-axis
body = body.faces(">X").workplane().circle(R_hole).cutThruAll()
# Intersecting bore through the T-branch (Y-axis)
body = body.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(R_hole).cutBlind(L_tube + L_t_boss + R_cyl)

# Final model
result = body

import cadquery as cq

# 1. Central forged block
# Dimensions: 14 along straight axis (X), 11 across (Y), 11 high (Z)
body = cq.Workplane("XY").box(14, 11, 11).edges().fillet(0.5)

# 2. Main straight axis (-X to +X)
# Straight cylinder body
straight_cyl = cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=-15).circle(4.5).extrude(30)
body = body.union(straight_cyl)

# Push-fit collar on -X end
collar = cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=-18).circle(5.5).extrude(3)
collar = collar.faces(">X or <X").edges().chamfer(0.5)
body = body.union(collar)

# Release ring on -X end
ring = cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=-20).circle(4.0).extrude(2)
ring = ring.faces("<X").edges().chamfer(0.4)
body = body.union(ring)

# Hex nut on +X end
nut1 = cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=15).polygon(6, 16).extrude(10)
# Create a cylinder to intersect with the hex nut to generate conical chamfers
cyl1 = cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=15).circle(8).extrude(10)
cyl1 = cyl1.faces(">X or <X").edges().chamfer(1.5)
nut1 = nut1.intersect(cyl1)
body = body.union(nut1)

# 3. Side branch axis (+Y)
# Branch cylinder body
branch_cyl = cq.Workplane("XZ").circle(4.5).extrude(15)
body = body.union(branch_cyl)

# Hex nut on +Y end
nut2 = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=15).polygon(6, 16).extrude(10)
# Create a cylinder to intersect with the hex nut to generate conical chamfers
cyl2 = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=15).circle(8).extrude(10)
cyl2 = cyl2.faces(">Y or <Y").edges().chamfer(1.5)
nut2 = nut2.intersect(cyl2)
body = body.union(nut2)

# 4. Internal fluid channels (Holes)
# Straight through-hole
body = body.cut(cq.Workplane("ZY").workplane(offset=-22).circle(3.175).extrude(50))

# Branch connecting hole
body = body.cut(cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-2).circle(3.175).extrude(30))

# Final result
result = body

import cadquery as cq

# Outer profile for a single solid branch (holes will be drilled later)
pts_outer = [
    (0, 0),
    (0, 5.5),
    (9, 5.5),
    (11, 9.5),
    (19, 9.5),
    (21, 6.0),
    (22, 6.0),
    (22, 4.75),
    (25, 4.75),
    (25, 0)
]

# Revolve the branch profile around the X-axis
core = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(pts_outer).close().revolve(360, (0,0,0), (1,0,0))

# Create a hexagonal prism to intersect with the core for the nut features
# A polygon of diameter 18.5 will correctly leave chamfered corners on the r=9.5 sections
hex_prism = cq.Workplane("YZ").polygon(6, 18.5).extrude(30)

# Intersect to form the chamfered hex nut on the branch
branch = core.intersect(hex_prism)

# Create the central forged body block with slightly filleted edges
body = cq.Workplane("XY").box(14, 14, 14).edges().fillet(0.5)

# Instantiate the three branches for the T-fitting
branch_x_pos = branch
branch_x_neg = branch.rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), 180)
branch_y_pos = branch.rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), 90)

# Combine all solid components
result = body.union(branch_x_pos).union(branch_x_neg).union(branch_y_pos)

# Create the internal fluid passages
# Main straight bore along the X-axis
hole_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(height=60, radius=3.5)

# Perpendicular bore along the Y-axis (only in +Y direction)
# Offset by 15mm with height 34mm means it extends from Y = -2 to Y = 32
# This ensures a clean intersection with the X-bore without piercing the flat back face (Y = -7)
hole_y = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=15).cylinder(height=34, radius=3.5)

# Cut the internal holes from the final geometry
result = result.cut(hole_x).cut(hole_y)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# --- Dimensions ---
# Central Block
L_block = 12.0

# Main Run (X axis)
D_main_cyl = 10.0
L_main_cyl = 6.0
W_main_hex = 16.0
L_main_hex = 8.0
D_main_tube = 8.0
L_main_tube = 2.5
D_main_hole = 6.0

# Branch Run (Y axis)
D_branch_cyl = 8.5
L_branch_cyl = 6.0
W_branch_hex = 14.0
L_branch_hex = 7.0
D_branch_tube = 6.0
L_branch_tube = 2.0
D_branch_hole = 4.0

# --- 1. Central Body ---
# Base forged block with filleted edges
res = cq.Workplane("XY").box(L_block, L_block, L_block).edges().fillet(0.6)

# Raised wrench pads on the unused faces (+Z, -Z, -Y)
pad_z = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=L_block/2).rect(6.0, 6.0).extrude(1.0)
pad_mz = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-L_block/2).rect(6.0, 6.0).extrude(-1.0)
pad_my = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-L_block/2).rect(6.0, 6.0).extrude(-1.0)

res = res.union(pad_z).union(pad_mz).union(pad_my)

# --- 2. Cylindrical Extensions ---
# Main cylinders along X
main_cyl = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-(L_block/2 + L_main_cyl)).circle(D_main_cyl/2).extrude(L_block + 2*L_main_cyl)
res = res.union(main_cyl)

# Branch cylinder along Y
branch_cyl = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=L_block/2).circle(D_branch_cyl/2).extrude(L_branch_cyl)
res = res.union(branch_cyl)

# --- 3. End Connectors (Hex + Tube) ---
def build_end_chamfered(across_flats, hex_length, tube_D, tube_length, rot_angle=0):
    D_c = across_flats / math.cos(math.pi/6)
    R_hex = D_c / 2.0
    R_flat = across_flats / 2.0
    chamfer_h = (R_hex - R_flat) * 1.2 # Conical chamfer depth
    
    # Base hex extrusion
    hex_nut = cq.Workplane("XY").polygon(6, D_c).extrude(hex_length)
    if rot_angle != 0:
        hex_nut = hex_nut.rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), rot_angle)
    
    # Profile for revolved cut to create the conical chamfers on the hex nut
    pts = [
        (0, 0),
        (R_flat, 0),
        (R_hex, chamfer_h),
        (R_hex, hex_length - chamfer_h),
        (R_flat, hex_length),
        (0, hex_length)
    ]
    revolved_cutter = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(pts).close().revolve(360, (0,0,0), (0,0,1))
    
    # Intersect to get perfectly chamfered hex ends
    hex_nut = hex_nut.intersect(revolved_cutter)
    
    # Protruding tube connection
    tube = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=hex_length).circle(tube_D/2.0).extrude(tube_length)
    tube = tube.edges(">Z").chamfer(0.3) # Outer tube chamfer
    
    return hex_nut.union(tube)

# Position and unite ends
pos_x = L_block/2 + L_main_cyl
# Main Right (+X)
end_px = build_end_chamfered(W_main_hex, L_main_hex, D_main_tube, L_main_tube, 30)
end_px = end_px.rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), 90).translate((pos_x, 0, 0))
res = res.union(end_px)

# Main Left (-X)
end_mx = build_end_chamfered(W_main_hex, L_main_hex, D_main_tube, L_main_tube, 30)
end_mx = end_mx.rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), -90).translate((-pos_x, 0, 0))
res = res.union(end_mx)

# Branch Top (+Y)
pos_y = L_block/2 + L_branch_cyl
end_py = build_end_chamfered(W_branch_hex, L_branch_hex, D_branch_tube, L_branch_tube, 0)
end_py = end_py.rotate((0,0,0), (1,0,0), -90).translate((0, pos_y, 0))
res = res.union(end_py)

# --- 4. Internal Passages ---
# Main through hole
main_cut = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-50).circle(D_main_hole/2).extrude(100)
res = res.cut(main_cut)

# Branch hole (stops at intersection)
branch_cut = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=0).circle(D_branch_hole/2).extrude(50)
res = res.cut(branch_cut)

# --- 5. Internal Hole Chamfers (Counterbores) ---
L_main_total = pos_x + L_main_hex + L_main_tube
L_branch_total = pos_y + L_branch_hex + L_branch_tube

# Lofted conical cuts for the internal flow path ends
chamfer_px = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=L_main_total-1.2).circle(D_main_hole/2)\
    .workplane(offset=1.2).circle(D_main_hole/2 + 0.8).loft()
res = res.cut(chamfer_px)

chamfer_mx = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-L_main_total+1.2).circle(D_main_hole/2)\
    .workplane(offset=-1.2).circle(D_main_hole/2 + 0.8).loft()
res = res.cut(chamfer_mx)

chamfer_py = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=L_branch_total-1.2).circle(D_branch_hole/2)\
    .workplane(offset=1.2).circle(D_branch_hole/2 + 0.8).loft()
res = res.cut(chamfer_py)

# Final Output Geometry
result = res

import cadquery as cq

def make_branch_external(hex_angle):
    # Neck
    part = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(4.5).extrude(14.0)
    
    # Hex Nut
    part = (
        part.faces(">Z")
        .workplane()
        .transformed(rotate=(0, 0, hex_angle))
        .polygon(6, 16.0)
        .extrude(8.0)
    )
    
    # Chamfer the outer face of the nut
    part = part.edges(">Z").chamfer(1.2)
    
    # Protruding tip
    part = part.faces(">Z").workplane().circle(5.2).extrude(2.0)
    
    return part

# Create central cross-body
center = cq.Workplane("ZY").cylinder(14.0, 6.5)

# Cut flats on top and bottom of the central body
cut_top = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=4.5).rect(20.0, 20.0).extrude(5.0)
cut_bot = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-4.5).rect(20.0, 20.0).extrude(-5.0)
center = center.cut(cut_top).cut(cut_bot)

# Create and position the three branches
branch_x_pos = make_branch_external(30.0).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), 90.0)
branch_x_neg = make_branch_external(30.0).rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), -90.0)
branch_y_pos = make_branch_external(0.0).rotate((0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0), -90.0)

# Combine external geometry
result = center.union(branch_x_pos).union(branch_x_neg).union(branch_y_pos)

# Hollow out the inside through each branch to form a connected cavity
# X+ Branch Cut
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().circle(3.5).cutBlind(-32.0)
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().circle(4.5).cutBlind(-3.0)

# X- Branch Cut
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().circle(3.5).cutBlind(-32.0)
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().circle(4.5).cutBlind(-3.0)

# Y+ Branch Cut
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(3.5).cutBlind(-32.0)
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(4.5).cutBlind(-3.0)

import cadquery as cq

# Key dimensions inferred from proportions
d_main = 14.0
l_main = 24.0
d_branch = 14.0
l_branch = 12.0
pad_size_x = 10.0
pad_size_y = 10.0
pad_h = 15.5
d_pipe = 10.0
l_pipe = 15.0
d_hole = 7.0

# Central block components
core_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(l_main, d_main/2)
# XZ workplane normal is -Y, offset -l_branch/2 moves it to +Y
core_y = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-l_branch/2).cylinder(l_branch, d_branch/2)
pad = cq.Workplane("XY").box(pad_size_x, pad_size_y, pad_h)

# Plain pipe section
pipe = (cq.Workplane("YZ")
        .workplane(offset=l_main/2 + l_pipe/2)
        .cylinder(l_pipe, d_pipe/2)
        .edges(">X").chamfer(0.5))

def make_nut():
    # Outer nut body (Hex width across flats ~17mm -> circumscribed dia 19.6)
    nut_body = (cq.Workplane("XY")
                .polygon(6, 19.6)
                .extrude(8.0)
                .faces(">Z").workplane()
                .circle(13.5/2)
                .extrude(6.0))
    
    # Chamfer outer edge of the cylindrical part
    nut_body = nut_body.edges(">Z and %CIRCLE").chamfer(1.2)
    
    # Cut internal clearance for tube, creating a visible gap
    nut_body = nut_body.faces("<Z").workplane().circle(10.5/2).cutThruAll()
    
    # Internal tube section (flush with the end of the nut)
    tube = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(d_pipe/2).extrude(14.0)
    
    return nut_body.union(tube)

# Create and position compression nuts
nut1 = make_nut().rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), -90).translate((-l_main/2, 0, 0))
nut2 = make_nut().rotate((0,0,0), (1,0,0), -90).translate((0, l_branch, 0))

# Assemble solid outer body
result = core_x.union(core_y).union(pad).union(pipe).union(nut1).union(nut2)

# Create internal channel cutters
hole_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(100.0, d_hole/2)
# Offset correctly to cut through the branch and intersect main channel without piercing back wall
hole_y = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-25.0).cylinder(54.0, d_hole/2)

# Cut internal channels through the entire assembly
result = result.cut(hole_x).cut(hole_y)

import cadquery as cq

# Profile points for the vertical body (revolved around global Z axis)
pts_v = [

import cadquery as cq

def solid_arm(rot_z=0):
    # Base profile: neck and conical transition
    profile = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
               .lineTo(4.5, 0)
               .lineTo(4.5, 12)
               .lineTo(7.5, 16)
               .lineTo(0, 16)
               .close())
    arm_base = profile.revolve(360, (0,0,0), (0,0,1))
    
    # Hex nut at the end
    hex_nut = (cq.Workplane("XY")
               .workplane(offset=16)
               .polygon(6, 16)
               .extrude(10)
               .edges(">Z").chamfer(0.8))
    
    arm = arm_base.union(hex_nut)
    
    # Align hexagon flats based on final orientation
    if rot_z != 0:
        arm = arm.rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), rot_z)
        
    return arm

# 1. Solid Center Block (wrench flats)
body = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(14, 12).extrude(6.5, both=True)
body = body.edges("|Z").fillet(1.5)

# 2. Create the three arms
# X arm: rot_z=30 makes flats parallel to Z after 90 deg rotation around Y
arm_x = solid_arm(rot_z=30).rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), 90)

# -X arm: similarly oriented
arm_nx = solid_arm(rot_z=30).rotate((0,0,0), (0,1,0), -90)

# Y arm (branch): rot_z=0 keeps flats parallel to X after -90 deg rotation around X
arm_y = solid_arm(rot_z=0).rotate((0,0,0), (1,0,0), -90)

# 3. Union all solid components
result = body.union(arm_x).union(arm_nx).union(arm_y)

# 4. Cut internal holes
# Main run through-hole along X axis
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().circle(3.0).cutThruAll()

# Branch hole along Y axis (blind cut to exactly meet the center)
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(3.0).cutBlind(-26.0)

# 5. Cut internal stepped recesses at all three ends
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().circle(4.25).cutBlind(-4.0)
result = result.faces(">X").workplane().circle(4.25).cutBlind(-4.0)
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().circle(4.25).cutBlind(-4.0)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters inferred from proportions
D_center = 28.0
H_center = 28.0
Chamfer_center = 1.5

D_base = 20.0
D_flange = 24.0
L_base = 19.0
L_total = 24.0
Outer_chamfer = 1.0

Bore_dia = 15.0
Inner_chamfer = 1.5
bore_depth = L_total + 5.0 

Hex_af = 14.0
Hex_depth = 6.0
hex_diam = Hex_af / math.cos(math.pi / 6)

Square_size = 16.0
Square_height = 2.0

# 1. Central Body
body = cq.Workplane("XY").cylinder(H_center, D_center/2.0)
body = body.edges("Z or -Z").chamfer(Chamfer_center)

# Add top and bottom square bosses
boss_top = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=H_center/2.0).rect(Square_size, Square_size).extrude(Square_height)
boss_bot = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-H_center/2.0).rect(Square_size, Square_size).extrude(-Square_height)
body = body.union(boss_top).union(boss_bot)

# 2. Branches exterior
def make_exterior_branch(direction):
    if direction == "+X":
        wp = cq.Workplane("YZ")
        sign = 1
        face_sel = ">X"
    elif direction == "-X":
        wp = cq.Workplane("YZ")
        sign = -1
        face_sel = "<X"
    elif direction == "+Y":
        wp = cq.Workplane("XZ")
        sign = 1
        face_sel = ">Y"
    elif direction == "-Y":
        wp = cq.Workplane("XZ")
        sign = -1
        face_sel = "<Y"
        
    base = wp.circle(D_base/2.0).extrude(sign * L_base)
    flange = wp.workplane(offset=sign * L_base).circle(D_flange/2.0).extrude(sign * (L_total - L_base))
    res = base.union(flange)
    res = res.faces(face_sel).edges().chamfer(Outer_chamfer)
    return res

body = body.union(make_exterior_branch("+X"))
body = body.union(make_exterior_branch("-X"))
body = body.union(make_exterior_branch("+Y"))
body = body.union(make_exterior_branch("-Y"))

# 3. Inner bores with chamfer
def make_bore_cutter(direction):
    bore_pts = [
        (0, 0),
        (-bore_depth, 0),
        (-bore_depth, Bore_dia/2.0),
        (-Inner_chamfer, Bore_dia/2.0),
        (0, Bore_dia/2.0 + Inner_chamfer)
    ]
    cutter = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(bore_pts).close().revolve(360, (0,0,0), (1,0,0))
    
    if direction == "+X":
        return cutter.translate((L_total, 0, 0))
    elif direction == "+Y":
        return cutter.rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), 90).translate((0, L_total, 0))
    elif direction == "-Y":
        return cutter.rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), -90).translate((0, -L_total, 0))

body = body.cut(make_bore_cutter("+X"))
body = body.cut(make_bore_cutter("+Y"))
body = body.cut(make_bore_cutter("-Y"))

# 4. Hexagonal plug recess
hex_cutter = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-L_total).polygon(6, hex_diam).extrude(Hex_depth)
body = body.cut(hex_cutter)

result = body

import cadquery as cq
import math

# --- Parameters ---
# Central body dimensions
box_s = 30.0
fillet_r = 5.0
face_off = box_s / 2.0

# Hex Port (+X axis)
hex_af = 24.0
# CadQuery polygon uses circum-diameter
hex_circ = hex_af / math.cos(math.radians(30))
hex_l = 5.0

# Simple Port (-X axis)
sp_od = 16.0
sp_l = 10.0

# Collar Ports (+Y and -Y axes)
cp_base_od = 16.0
cp_base_l = 3.0
cp_collar_od = 22.0
cp_collar_l = 2.0
cp_tip_od = 16.0
cp_tip_l = 4.0

# General dimensions
hole_d = 11.0
chamfer_size = 0.5

# --- Geometry Construction ---

# 1. Base central body (Box with filleted edges)
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(box_s, box_s, box_s).edges().fillet(fillet_r)

# 2. Add Hex Port (Solid plug) on +X face
result = (
    result.faces(">X")
    .workplane()
    .polygon(6, hex_circ)
    .extrude(hex_l)
    .faces(">X")
    .edges()
    .chamfer(chamfer_size)
)

# 3. Add Simple Port on -X face
result = (
    result.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(sp_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(sp_l)
    .faces("<X")
    .edges()
    .chamfer(chamfer_size)
)

# 4. Add Collar Port on +Y face
result = (
    result.faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_base_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_base_l)
    .faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_collar_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_collar_l)
    .faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_tip_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_tip_l)
    .faces(">Y")
    .edges()
    .chamfer(chamfer_size)
)

# 5. Add Collar Port on -Y face
result = (
    result.faces("<Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_base_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_base_l)
    .faces("<Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_collar_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_collar_l)
    .faces("<Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(cp_tip_od / 2.0)
    .extrude(cp_tip_l)
    .faces("<Y")
    .edges()
    .chamfer(chamfer_size)
)

# 6. Cut internal passages (Holes)
# Y-axis hole (goes completely through both collar ports)
result = (
    result.faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .circle(hole_d / 2.0)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# X-axis hole (starts at simple port, goes to the center to intersect Y hole, leaves Hex plug solid)
depth_x = sp_l + (box_s / 2.0) + 1.0 # Extra 1.0mm to ensure clean intersection at center
result = (
    result.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(hole_d / 2.0)
    .cutBlind(depth_x)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Dimensional parameters inferred from proportions
D_main = 18.0
L_main_cyl = 36.0
D_forge = 26.0
L_forge_X = 22.0
L_forge_Y = 16.0
W_flat = 18.0

W_main_hex = 25.4
L_main_hex = 12.0
D_main_hex_circum = W_main_hex * 1.1547005 # Circumscribed diameter for hexagon

W_branch_hex = 22.2
L_branch_hex = 7.0
D_branch_hex_circum = W_branch_hex * 1.1547005

D_flange = 21.0
L_flange = 2.5
D_groove = 16.5
L_groove = 3.5
D_end = 19.0
L_end = 6.0

D_hole_main = 10.0
D_hole_branch = 10.0
D_bore_main = 17.0
L_bore_main = 14.0
D_bore_branch = 13.0
L_bore_branch = 5.0

# 1. Main cylindrical body
main_X = cq.Workplane("ZY").cylinder(L_main_cyl, D_main/2)

# 2. Central Forging with wrench flats
forge_X = cq.Workplane("ZY").cylinder(L_forge_X, D_forge/2)
forge_Y = cq.Workplane("XZ").circle(D_forge/2).extrude(L_forge_Y)
forging = forge_X.union(forge_Y)
# Intersect with a bounding box to create the parallel wrench flats
forging = forging.intersect(cq.Workplane("XY").box(L_forge_X + 10, L_forge_Y * 2 + 10, W_flat))

# 3. Combine body base components
body = main_X.union(forging)

# 4. End Hex Nuts on main body
left_hex = (cq.Workplane("ZY")
            .workplane(offset=-L_main_cyl/2)
            .polygon(6, D_main_hex_circum)
            .extrude(-L_main_hex)
           )
right_hex = (cq.Workplane("ZY")
             .workplane(offset=L_main_cyl/2)
             .polygon(6, D_main_hex_circum)
             .extrude(L_main_hex)
            )
body = body.union(left_hex).union(right_hex)

# 5. Stepped Branch Features
branch = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
          .workplane(offset=L_forge_Y)
          .polygon(6, D_branch_hex_circum)
          .extrude(L_branch_hex)
          .faces(">Y")
          .circle(D_flange/2)
          .extrude(L_flange)
          .faces(">Y")
          .circle(D_groove/2)
          .extrude(L_groove)
          .faces(">Y")
          .circle(D_end/2)
          .extrude(L_end)
         )
body = body.union(branch)

# 6. Apply outer chamfers before drilling to avoid internal edge selection
body = body.edges(">X").chamfer(1.5)
body = body.edges("<X").chamfer(1.5)
body = body.edges(">Y").chamfer(1.0)

# 7. Internal Bores & Through Holes
# Countersink hole cuts the bore diameter along with an entry chamfer
body = body.faces("<X").workplane().cskHole(D_bore_main, D_bore_main+2.5, 90, depth=L_bore_main)
body = body.faces(">X").workplane().cskHole(D_bore_main, D_bore_main+2.5, 90, depth=L_bore_main)
body = body.faces(">Y").workplane().cskHole(D_bore_branch, D_bore_branch+2.0, 90, depth=L_bore_branch)

# Extrude cuts from center planes clear out through-holes leaving a realistic drilled conical step
body = body.workplane("ZY").circle(D_hole_main/2).extrude(100, both=True, combine="cut")
body = body.workplane("XZ").circle(D_hole_branch/2).extrude(100, both=True, combine="cut")

# Final assigned variable
result = body

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
hub_size = 15.0
hub_chamfer = 2.0
boss_size = 11.0
boss_chamfer = 1.5
boss_extrude = 1.5

hex_af = 17.0
hex_d = hex_af / 0.866025  # Circumscribed circle diameter for polygon
hex_l_f = 13.0             # Female branch hex length
hex_l_m = 7.0              # Male branch hex length

m_cyl1_d = 14.5
m_cyl1_l = 3.0
m_groove_d = 10.5
m_groove_l = 1.8
m_cyl2_d = 13.0
m_cyl2_l = 6.0
m_tip_chamfer = 1.0

hole_f_d = 11.0
hole_f_csk = 13.0
hole_m_d = 7.5
hole_m_csk = 9.5

# 1. Central Hub
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(hub_size, hub_size, hub_size)
result = result.edges("|Z").chamfer(hub_chamfer)

# 2. Top and Bottom Bosses
boss_base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(boss_size, boss_size, hub_size + 2 * boss_extrude)
boss_base = boss_base.edges("|Z").chamfer(boss_chamfer)
result = result.union(boss_base)

# 3. Branches
# X+ Female Branch
branch_xp = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=hub_size / 2).polygon(6, hex_d).extrude(hex_l_f)
result = result.union(branch_xp)

# X- Female Branch
branch_xn = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=-hub_size / 2).polygon(6, hex_d).extrude(-hex_l_f)
result = result.union(branch_xn)

# Y- Female Branch
branch_yn = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-hub_size / 2).polygon(6, hex_d).extrude(-hex_l_f)
result = result.union(branch_yn)

# Y+ Male Branch
b_yp_hex = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=hub_size / 2).polygon(6, hex_d).extrude(hex_l_m)
b_yp_c1 = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=hub_size / 2 + hex_l_m).circle(m_cyl1_d / 2).extrude(m_cyl1_l)
b_yp_g = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=hub_size / 2 + hex_l_m + m_cyl1_l).circle(m_groove_d / 2).extrude(m_groove_l)
b_yp_c2 = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=hub_size / 2 + hex_l_m + m_cyl1_l + m_groove_l).circle(m_cyl2_d / 2).extrude(m_cyl2_l)

male_branch = b_yp_hex.union(b_yp_c1).union(b_yp_g).union(b_yp_c2)
male_branch = male_branch.faces(">Y").edges().chamfer(m_tip_chamfer)
result = result.union(male_branch)

# 4. Inner Holes
depth_f = hub_size / 2 + hex_l_f + 2.0
depth_m = hub_size / 2 + hex_l_m + m_cyl1_l + m_groove_l + m_cyl2_l + 2.0

result = result.faces(">X").workplane().cskHole(hole_f_d, cskDiameter=hole_f_csk, cskAngle=90, depth=depth_f)
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().cskHole(hole_f_d, cskDiameter=hole_f_csk, cskAngle=90, depth=depth_f)
result = result.faces("<Y").workplane().cskHole(hole_f_d, cskDiameter=hole_f_csk, cskAngle=90, depth=depth_f)
result = result.faces(">Y").workplane().cskHole(hole_m_d, cskDiameter=hole_m_csk, cskAngle=90, depth=depth_m)

import cadquery as cq

# Define dimensions and geometry
# 1. Central T-Body profile and extrusion
t_pts = [
    (14.0, 7.0),
    (14.0, -7.0),
    (-14.0, -7.0),
    (-14.0, 7.0),
    (-7.0, 7.0),
    (-7.0, 14.0),
    (7.0, 14.0),
    (7.0, 7.0)
]
body = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(t_pts).close().extrude(7.0, both=True)

# 2. Main Barrel section
barrel = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=14.0).circle(14.0 / 2.0).extrude(28.0)

# 3. Hex Nut flange
hex_nut = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=42.0).polygon(6, 22.0).extrude(5.0)

# 4. Flare End (Threaded AN fitting end) profile to be revolved
flare_pts = [
    (0, 0),
    (13.5 / 2.0, 0),
    (13.5 / 2.0, 2.0),
    (10.0 / 2.0, 2.0),
    (10.0 / 2.0, 3.5),
    (12.0 / 2.0, 3.5),
    (12.0 / 2.0, 11.5),
    (9.0 / 2.0, 15.5),
    (0, 15.5)
]
flare_profile = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(flare_pts).close()
flare_solid = flare_profile.revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))

# Position the three flare ends onto the correct faces
left_end = flare_solid.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), -90).translate((-14.0, 0, 0))
right_end = flare_solid.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), 90).translate((47.0, 0, 0))
branch_end = flare_solid.rotate((0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0), -90).translate((0, 14.0, 0))

# 5. Assemble all solid components
result = (
    body
    .union(barrel)
    .union(hex_nut)
    .union(left_end.val())
    .union(right_end.val())
    .union(branch_end.val())
)

# 6. Cut the through-holes for fluid flow
main_hole = cq.Workplane("YZ").circle(6.0 / 2.0).extrude(200.0, both=True)
branch_hole = cq.Workplane("XZ").circle(6.0 / 2.0).extrude(200.0, both=True)

result = result.cut(main_hole).cut(branch_hole)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Model proportions and dimensions inferred from the multi-view image
D = 14.0       # Main diameter
L1 = 60.0      # Bottom cylinder length (up to the groove)
L2 = 24.0      # Top cylinder length (above the groove)
d = 9.5        # Top protruding cylinder diameter
l = 4.0        # Top protruding cylinder straight length
r = d / 2.0    # Top dome radius (hemisphere)

# Groove dimensions
gw = 0.4       # Groove width
gd = 0.4       # Groove depth

# Arc calculations for the top dome
Y_top = L1 + L2 + l
mid_x = r * math.sqrt(2.0) / 2.0
mid_y = Y_top + r * math.sqrt(2.0) / 2.0
end_x = 0.0
end_y = Y_top + r

# Construct the geometry by revolving a 2D cross-section profile
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0.0, 0.0)
    .lineTo(D/2.0, 0.0)
    .lineTo(D/2.0, L1 - gw/2.0)
    .lineTo(D/2.0 - gd, L1)
    .lineTo(D/2.0, L1 + gw/2.0)
    .lineTo(D/2.0, L1 + L2)
    .lineTo(d/2.0, L1 + L2)
    .lineTo(d/2.0, Y_top)
    .threePointArc((mid_x, mid_y), (end_x, end_y))
    .close()
    .revolve(360.0, (0.0, 0.0), (0.0, 1.0))
)

import cadquery as cq

# Define the points for the half-profile of the stepped pin
# The profile is drawn on the XZ workplane, where the workplane's local Y-axis 
# maps to the global Z-axis. We will revolve around this local Y-axis.
profile_points = [
    (0.0, 0.0),     # Start at bottom center
    (6.0, 0.0),     # Bottom flat face
    (6.0, 46.0),    # Main base cylinder
    (4.8, 50.0),    # Conical transition to middle cylinder
    (4.8, 74.0),    # Middle cylinder
    (3.0, 74.0),    # Flat step/shoulder inward
    (3.0, 78.0),    # Top pin cylinder
    (0.0, 78.0)     # Top flat center (will be filleted into a dome)
]

# Create the model by drawing the profile, revolving it, and filleting the top
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polyline(profile_points)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0))
    # Apply a fillet to the topmost edge. A radius of 2.99 on a cylinder 
    # of radius 3.0 creates a near-perfect hemisphere (dome) while 
    # avoiding topological singularities at the exact center.
    .edges(">Z")
    .fillet(2.99)
)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Proportional dimensions inferred from the views
L = 60.0           # Length of the cylinder
D = 20.0           # Diameter of the cylinder
R = D / 2.0        # Radius
W = 5.0            # Width of the tapered slot
L_slot = 40.0      # Length of the slot along the cylinder
depth_start = 5.0  # Depth of the slot at the flat end face

# Calculate the angle of the slot's flat bottom
dy = depth_start
dx = L_slot
angle = math.degrees(math.atan2(dy, dx))

# Create the main cylindrical body along the X-axis
cyl = cq.Workplane("YZ").circle(R).extrude(L)

# Create a custom workplane for the cutting tool
# Offset sets the start point of the cut, rotate tilts it to form the taper
cutter_wp = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, R - depth_start, 0), rotate=(0, 0, angle))
)

# Build a rectangular box to subtract from the cylinder
# The box is sized to completely cover the top half of the cylinder
cutter = (
    cutter_wp
    .center(L, 10)         # Shift center so the cut starts exactly at X=0

    .lineTo(r_head, h_head)

    (1.7, 3.7),           # Groove taper start
    (1.7, 4.1),           # Groove flat bottom
    (2.0, 4.3),           # Groove taper end (shaft continues)

    (0.0, 16.0)           # Center point of the tip
]

]

# Create the cutting tool for the recess
# Extruding with a positive taper shrinks the profile as it goes deeper, characteristic of a standard screw drive
tool = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=-0.1)  # Offset slightly to ensure a clean boolean cut at the Z=0 plane without coplanar face issues
    .polyline(cross_pts)
    .close()
    .extrude(2.1, taper=12.0)  # Total extrusion 2.1mm achieves a net 2.0mm depth into the head
)

# Produce final geometry by subtracting the tool from the body
result = body.cut(tool)

# Base dimensions

l_head = 5.0

r_head = d_head / 2.0

        .circle(r_head)
        .workplane(offset=-l_head)
        .circle(r_shank)
        .loft())

         .circle(r_shank)
         .extrude(-(l_shank - chamfer_size)))

tip = (cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, -(l_total - chamfer_size)))
       .circle(r_shank)
       .workplane(offset=-chamfer_size)
       .circle(r_shank - chamfer_size)
       .loft())

# Combine the solid components
base = head.union(shank).union(tip)

# Cross recess dimensions

cross_dep = 3.0

hl = cross_len / 2.0

    (hl, -hw), (hw, -hw), (hw, -hl), (-hw, -hl),
    (-hw, -hw), (-hl, -hw), (-hl, hw), (-hw, hw)
]

# Create the cross recess cut on the top flat face
result = (base.faces(">Z").workplane()
          .polyline(cross_pts).close()
          .cutBlind(-cross_dep))

D_cross = 1.6

p3_end = (R_shaft, -(H_flare + H_groove))
p4 = (R_shaft, -(H_total - H_chamfer))

    .moveTo(*p0)

    .rect(W_cross, L_cross)

)

result = (
    body.faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .placeSketch(cross_sketch)

body = body_profile.revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))

# Function to calculate thread inner and outer radii, tapering along the tip
def get_radii(z):
    if z >= -14.0:
        return 2.0, 3.8
    else:
        frac = (z + 19.0) / 5.0
        if frac < 0: frac = 0
        return (2.25 * frac) - 0.2, 3.8 * frac

# Add threads (approximated as robust, distinct angled rings)
z_centers = [-5.5, -8.5, -11.5, -14.5]
for z_c in z_centers:
    in_r, out_r = get_radii(z_c)
    ring_profile = (
        cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .moveTo(in_r, z_c + 0.8)
        .lineTo(out_r, z_c + 0.1)
        .lineTo(out_r, z_c - 0.1)
        .lineTo(in_r, z_c - 0.8)
        .close()
    )
    body = body.union(ring_profile.revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)))

# Create robust, tapered cutting tools for the Phillips drive
wp = cq.Workplane("XY")
cross1 = wp.workplane(offset=0.5).rect(4.0, 0.6).workplane(offset=4.0).rect(7.0, 1.5).loft()
cross2 = wp.workplane(offset=0.5).rect(0.6, 4.0).workplane(offset=4.0).rect(1.5, 7.0).loft()
center_cone = wp.workplane(offset=-0.5).circle(0.1).workplane(offset=5.0).circle(1.5).loft()

# Subtract the drive geometry from the main body
result = body.cut(cross1).cut(cross2).cut(center_cone)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Dimensions for the wood screw
head_dia = 9.0
shank_dia = 4.0
core_dia = 2.8
thread_outer_dia = 6.0

head_height = 2.5
shank_length = 3.0
straight_thread_length = 17.0
tapered_thread_length = 10.0

# 1. Create the base core of the screw
core_pts = [
    (0, 0),
    (head_dia / 2, 0),
    (shank_dia / 2, -head_height),
    (shank_dia / 2, -(head_height + shank_length)),
    (core_dia / 2, -(head_height + shank_length + 1.0)), # slight transition to core
    (core_dia / 2, -(head_height + shank_length + straight_thread_length)),
    (0, -(head_height + shank_length + straight_thread_length + tapered_thread_length))
]
core = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(core_pts).close().revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0))

# 2. Cut the Phillips cross recess
cross_len = 5.5
cross_width = 1.2
cross_depth = 2.0
taper_angle = 12

cross1 = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(cross_len, cross_width).extrude(-cross_depth, taper=taper_angle)
cross2 = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(cross_width, cross_len).extrude(-cross_depth, taper=taper_angle)
core = core.cut(cross1).cut(cross2)

# 3. Create the thread bounding envelope
# This envelope trims the outer edge of the thread to form the correct profile and tip
env_pts = [
    (0, -5.5),
    (shank_dia / 2, -5.5),
    (thread_outer_dia / 2, -7.5),
    (thread_outer_dia / 2, -(head_height + shank_length + straight_thread_length)),
    (0, -(head_height + shank_length + straight_thread_length + tapered_thread_length))
]
thread_envelope = cq.Workplane("XZ").polyline(env_pts).close().revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0))

# 4. Generate the helical path for the thread
pitch = 2.0
start_z = -5.0
end_z = -33.0
total_z = end_z - start_z
turns = abs(total_z) / pitch

def straight_helix(t):
    theta = t * turns * 2 * math.pi
    z = start_z + t * total_z
    r = core_dia / 2
    return (r * math.cos(theta), r * math.sin(theta), z)

path_wp = cq.Workplane("XY").parametricCurve(straight_helix, N=600)

# 5. Define and sweep the thread profile
# The profile is drawn on the normal plane mapped by Frenet sweep
# X maps to the inward normal, Y maps to the binormal (roughly vertical)
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polyline([
        (1.399, 0.8),    # Inner base top (reaches deep into the core)
        (-1.8, 0.05),    # Outer tip top
        (-1.8, -0.05),   # Outer tip bottom
        (1.399, -0.8)    # Inner base bottom
    ]).close()
)

# Sweep the profile along the helix
thread_sweep = profile.sweep(path_wp, isFrenet=True)

# 6. Trim the continuous thread with the envelope and combine with the core
thread = thread_sweep.intersect(thread_envelope)
result = core.union(thread)

import cadquery as cq
from math import cos, sin, pi

# General dimensions
core_r = 1.75
head_r = 4.8
thread_r = 2.6
pitch = 1.6

# 1. Base Body (Head, Shaft, and Drill Tip)
body_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 2.5)
    .threePointArc((3.0, 2.0), (head_r, 0.6))
    .lineTo(head_r, 0)
    .lineTo(core_r, 0)
    .lineTo(core_r, -10.5) # Shaft core to drill straight section
    .lineTo(0, -12.5)      # Conical drill tip
    .close()
)
body = body_profile.revolve()

# 2. Helical Thread
z_start = -1.0
z_end = -9.0
length = z_start - z_end
turns = length / pitch

# Define the thread path
path = cq.Workplane("XY").parametricCurve(
    lambda t: (core_r * cos(t * 2 * pi * turns), core_r * sin(t * 2 * pi * turns), z_start - t * length),
    N=300, makeWire=True
)

# Define the thread cross-section profile
thread_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-0.2, 0.4)
    .lineTo(thread_r - core_r, 0)
    .lineTo(-0.2, -0.4)
    .close()
)
thread = thread_profile.sweep(path)

# Envelope to nicely taper the thread ends
thread_envelope = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, -0.5)
    .lineTo(core_r - 0.1, -0.5)
    .lineTo(thread_r, -1.5)
    .lineTo(thread_r, -7.0)
    .lineTo(core_r - 0.1, -8.5)
    .lineTo(0, -8.5)
    .close()
    .revolve()
)
thread = thread.intersect(thread_envelope)
body = body.union(thread)

# 3. Drill Point Flutes
# Sweeping a tapered cut from the tip upwards to create fading drill flutes
cutter = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=-13.5)
    .center(1.6, 1.6)
    .circle(1.8)
    .extrude(6.0, taper=12)
)
body = body.cut(cutter).cut(cutter.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 180))

# 4. Phillips Drive Recess
# Two crossed rectangular cuts with taper
philips_cut1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=2.6)
    .rect(1.2, 5.5)
    .extrude(-2.5, taper=15)
)
philips_cut2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=2.6)
    .rect(5.5, 1.2)
    .extrude(-2.5, taper=15)
)
body = body.cut(philips_cut1).cut(philips_cut2)

# Final result
result = body

import cadquery as cq
import math

# --- Parameters inferred from proportions ---
L_tot = 25.4         # Total length
D_hd = 8.2           # Head diameter
H_hd = 2.8           # Head thickness (countersink depth)
L_unth = 3.0         # Unthreaded shaft length below head
L_th_str = 13.6      # Straight threaded section length
L_tip = 6.0          # Tapered tip length

D_core = 3.5         # Shaft core diameter
D_th = 5.4           # Thread outer diameter
pitch = 1.6          # Thread pitch

R_core = D_core / 2.0
R_th = D_th / 2.0

# --- 1. Base Core Geometry ---
core_pts = [
    (0, 0),
    (D_hd / 2.0, 0),
    (R_core, -H_hd),
    (R_core, -(H_hd + L_unth + L_th_str)),
    (0.01, -L_tot),  # Slight offset to avoid singularity at the exact tip
    (0, -L_tot)
]
core = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .polyline(core_pts)
        .close()
        .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)))

# --- 2. Helical Thread Path ---
Z_th_start = -(H_hd + L_unth)
L_th_tot = L_th_str + L_tip
turns = L_th_tot / pitch

path_pts = []
steps = int(turns * 60)
for i in range(steps + 1):
    t = i / steps
    a = t * turns * 2.0 * math.pi
    z = Z_th_start - L_th_tot * t

    # Taper the path radius at the tip
    if z >= -(H_hd + L_unth + L_th_str):
        r = R_core
    else:
        t_tip = (-(H_hd + L_unth + L_th_str) - z) / L_tip
        r = R_core * (1.0 - t_tip)

    r = max(r, 0.05)  # Prevent absolute zero radius for the sweep path
    path_pts.append(cq.Vector(r * math.cos(a), r * math.sin(a), z))

helix = cq.Wire.assembleEdges([cq.Edge.makeSpline(path_pts)])

# --- 3. Thread Profile ---
pen = 0.3  # Penetration into the core to ensure a clean boolean union
th_depth = R_th - R_core
w = pitch * 0.75

profile = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
           .center(R_core, Z_th_start)
           .moveTo(-pen, w / 2.0)
           .lineTo(th_depth + 0.2, 0)  # Extend slightly past final outer radius
           .lineTo(-pen, -w / 2.0)
           .close())

# Sweep the profile along the helical path
raw_thread = profile.sweep(helix, isFrenet=True)

# --- 4. Bounding Envelope to trim the thread ---
# This creates a smooth thread fade-in at the top and perfectly tapers it at the tip
env_pts = [
    (0, Z_th_start + 0.2),
    (R_core, Z_th_start),
    (R_th, Z_th_start - pitch),
    (R_th, -(H_hd + L_unth + L_th_str)),
    (0.01, -L_tot),
    (0, -L_tot)
]
envelope = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
            .polyline(env_pts)
            .close()
            .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)))

thread = raw_thread.intersect(envelope)

# --- 5. Phillips Recess ---
rec_d = 1.8
cross1 = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(4.8, 1.2).extrude(-rec_d, taper=8)
cross2 = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(1.2, 4.8).extrude(-rec_d, taper=8)
recess = cross1.union(cross2)

# --- 6. Final Assembly ---
result = core.union(thread).cut(recess)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# --- Parameters ---
# Dimensions inferred from the multi-view image proportions
D_head = 7.0
T_head = 2.6
L_shank = 12.0
L_tip = 4.0
P = 1.5
R_core = 1.2
R_thread = 2.2
L_tot = L_shank + L_tip

# --- Thread Path ---
# Define a continuous helical path for the thread, tapering at the tip
def screw_path(r_start, r_tip, L_s, L_t, pitch):
    turns_s = L_s / pitch
    turns_t = L_t / pitch
    total_turns = turns_s + turns_t
    total_L = L_s + L_t

    def helix(t):
        z = total_L * t
        current_turn = total_turns * t
        
        # Conical taper for the tip, straight for the shank
        if z < L_t:
            r = r_tip + (r_start - r_tip) * (z / L_t)
        else:
            r = r_start
            
        angle = 2 * math.pi * current_turn
        x = r * math.cos(angle)
        y = r * math.sin(angle)
        return (x, y, z)
        
    return helix

# Create the wire path with enough points to smoothly define multiple turns
path = cq.Workplane("XY").parametricCurve(
    screw_path(R_core, 0.1, L_shank, L_tip, P), N=250
)

# --- Thread Profile ---
thread_h = R_thread - R_core
thread_w = P * 0.6
overlap_core = 0.2

# Profile is swept along the path. 
# In a Frenet sweep along a helix, local +X maps to the normal pointing inward.
# So -X points outward to form the thread crest, and +X bites into the core.
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polygon([
        (overlap_core, -thread_w/2),
        (-thread_h, -thread_w/6),
        (-thread_h, thread_w/6),
        (overlap_core, thread_w/2)
    ])
)
thread = profile.sweep(path, isFrenet=True)

# --- Core Shank ---
core = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(0.1, 0)
    .lineTo(R_core, L_tip)
    .lineTo(R_core, L_tot)
    .lineTo(0, L_tot)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
)

# --- Pan Head ---
head = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(0, 0, L_tot))
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(D_head/2, 0)
    .lineTo(D_head/2, T_head * 0.25)
    .threePointArc((D_head/4, T_head * 0.9), (0, T_head))
    .lineTo(0, 0)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
)

# --- Phillips Drive ---
w_slot = 1.3
l_slot = 4.5
d_slot = 1.8
taper_angle = 15

# Two crossed, tapered rectangular slots
cross1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, L_tot + T_head + 0.1))
    .rect(l_slot, w_slot)
    .extrude(-(d_slot + 0.1), taper=taper_angle)
)
cross2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, L_tot + T_head + 0.1))
    .rect(w_slot, l_slot)
    .extrude(-(d_slot + 0.1), taper=taper_angle)
)

# --- Assembly ---
result = core.union(thread).union(head)
result = result.cut(cross1).cut(cross2)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Create the main body (shaft + pan head) via revolution
body = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .moveTo(0, -25.0)
        .lineTo(0.5, -25.0)
        .lineTo(0.8, -24.7)
        .lineTo(2.0, -20.0)
        .lineTo(2.0, 0)
        .lineTo(3.7, 0)
        .lineTo(4.0, 0.3)
        .lineTo(4.0, 1.92)
        .threePointArc((2.0, 2.746), (0, 3.0))
        .close()
        .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
       )

# Create the Torx T20-style drive cut
drive_wp = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=3.0)
torx_pts = []
steps = 72
r_out = 1.9
r_in = 1.2
R_avg = (r_out + r_in) / 2.0
A = (r_out - r_in) / 2.0

for i in range(steps):
    theta = 2 * math.pi * i / steps
    r = R_avg + A * math.cos(6 * theta)
    torx_pts.append((r * math.cos(theta), r * math.sin(theta)))

drive_profile = drive_wp.polyline(torx_pts).close()
body = body.cut(drive_profile.extrude(-1.8))

# Define the path for the helical thread
def helix_path(t):
    z = -2.0 - 22.5 * t
    angle = 2 * math.pi * 14.0 * t
    
    # Base radius follows the shaft taper
    if z >= -20.0:
        r = 1.8
    else:
        r = 1.8 - 1.0 * (-20.0 - z) / 5.0
        
    # Smooth runouts at both ends to submerge the thread into the shaft
    if t < 0.05:
        u = t / 0.05
        r = 0.1 + 1.7 * 0.5 * (1.0 - math.cos(u * math.pi))
    elif t > 0.95:
        u = (t - 0.95) / 0.05
        blend = 0.5 * (1.0 + math.cos(u * math.pi))
        r = 0.1 + (r - 0.1) * blend
        
    x = r * math.cos(angle)
    y = r * math.sin(angle)
    return (x, y, z)

path = cq.Workplane("XY").parametricCurve(helix_path, N=300)

# Define the thread cross-section profile
thread_profile = (cq.Workplane("XY")
                  .moveTo(0.2, -0.6)
                  .lineTo(-0.8, -0.15)
                  .lineTo(-0.8, 0.15)
                  .lineTo(0.2, 0.6)
                  .close())

# Sweep the thread along the helical path using a Frenet frame
thread = thread_profile.sweep(path, isFrenet=True)

# Union the body and thread into the final result
result = body.union(thread)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Calculate exact intermediate point for the pan head dome arc
# Dome radius R = 7.5, center at (0, 0, -4.5). Arc passes through (0, 3.0) and (4.5, 1.5).
# Finding z for x = 2.25
arc_z = math.sqrt(7.5**2 - 2.25**2) - 4.5

# 1. Base Revolve (Head and Shaft)
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 3.0)
    .threePointArc((2.25, arc_z), (4.5, 1.5))
    .lineTo(4.5, 0.0)
    .lineTo(2.0, 0.0)
    .lineTo(2.0, -13.0)
    .lineTo(0.0, -13.0)
    .close()
)
solid = profile.revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))

# 2. Wedge Tip Intersection
wedge_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-6.0, 5.0)
    .lineTo(6.0, 5.0)
    .lineTo(6.0, -9.0)
    .lineTo(0.6, -12.0)
    .lineTo(-0.6, -12.0)
    .lineTo(-6.0, -9.0)
    .close()
)
wedge_prism = wedge_profile.extrude(10.0, both=True)
solid = solid.intersect(wedge_prism)

# 3. Phillips Drive Cuts
# Create the cross sketch
s = cq.Sketch().rect(6.0, 1.2).rect(1.2, 6.0)

# Tapered cross recess, rotated 45 degrees to match the reference images
tool_cross = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=3.1)
    .placeSketch(s)
    .extrude(-2.2, taper=15.0)
    .rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 45.0)
)

# Central conical dimple for the Phillips drive
tool_cone = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=3.1)
    .circle(1.2)
    .extrude(-2.5, taper=25.0)
)

# Apply cuts to final geometry
result = solid.cut(tool_cross).cut(tool_cone)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Dimensions inferred from visual proportions
shank_d = 3.0
shank_r = shank_d / 2.0
shank_l = 8.0

head_d = 6.0
head_r = head_d / 2.0
head_h = 1.5

# Sphere parameters for the domed pan head
# Calculated so the sphere intersects exactly at head_r at the base (Z = shank_l)
sphere_r = 3.75
sphere_z = shank_l - (sphere_r - head_h)
top_z = shank_l + head_h

# 1. Base Geometry
shank = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(shank_r).extrude(shank_l)

# Create a spherical cap for the head by intersecting a bounding cylinder with a sphere
head_cyl = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=shank_l).circle(head_r).extrude(head_h)
head_sphere = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=sphere_z).sphere(sphere_r)
cap = head_cyl.intersect(head_sphere)

body = shank.union(cap)

# 2. Cross Recess (Phillips-style top drive)
recess_w = 1.2
recess_l = 5.0
taper_angle = 30.0

# Calculate exact depth to taper to a near-point, avoiding non-manifold vertices
recess_depth = (recess_w / 2.0 - 0.01) / math.tan(math.radians(taper_angle))
start_z = top_z + 0.05

# Create two perpendicular tapered wedges to form the cross recess
cut1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=start_z)
    .rect(recess_l, recess_w)
    .extrude(-recess_depth, taper=taper_angle)
)

    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=start_z)
    .rect(recess_w, recess_l)
    .extrude(-recess_depth, taper=taper_angle)
)

body = body.cut(cut1).cut(cut2)

# 3. Bottom Slot (Shank end)
slot_w = 1.2
slot_depth = 1.5
bottom_slot = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(slot_w, shank_d * 2.0).extrude(slot_depth)

# 4. Final Geometry
result = body.cut(bottom_slot)

# cutBlind with a positive depth cuts inwards (+Z)

import cadquery as cq

# Base dimensions inferred from proportions
shank_radius = 1.8
shank_length = 5.0
cone_length = 3.5
head_radius = 4.8
head_cyl_height = 1.3
head_dome_height = 1.3
cross_length = 5.5
cross_width = 1.2
cross_depth = 2.0

# Calculated Z coordinates for the revolve profile
z_tip = -(shank_length + cone_length)
z_cone_base = -shank_length
z_head_base = 0.0
z_head_cyl_top = head_cyl_height
z_head_top = head_cyl_height + head_dome_height

# Arc midpoint calculation for the domed head
# R_head = 4.8, h_dome = 1.3 -> Sphere Radius approx 9.51
# Midpoint at X = 2.4 -> Z approx 2.29
arc_mid_x = 2.4
arc_mid_z = 2.29

# Create the main body using a revolved profile
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, z_tip)
    .lineTo(shank_radius, z_cone_base)
    .lineTo(shank_radius, z_head_base)
    .lineTo(head_radius, z_head_base)
    .lineTo(head_radius, z_head_cyl_top)
    .threePointArc((arc_mid_x, arc_mid_z), (0, z_head_top))
    .close()
)

base = profile.revolve()

# Create the cutting tools for the Phillips recess
# We create them separately to ensure clean tapered geometry without wire intersection issues

# Tool 1: X-axis slot
tool1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, z_head_top + 0.1))
    .rect(cross_length, cross_width)
    .extrude(-(cross_depth + 0.1), taper=15)
)

# Tool 2: Y-axis slot
tool2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, z_head_top + 0.1))
    .rect(cross_width, cross_length)
    .extrude(-(cross_depth + 0.1), taper=15)
)

# Tool 3: Center conical impression typical of Phillips heads
tool3 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, z_head_top + 0.1))
    .circle(cross_width * 0.75)
    .extrude(-(cross_depth + 0.4), taper=25)
)

# Subtract the tools from the base body to get the final geometry
result = base.cut(tool1).cut(tool2).cut(tool3)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters for the screw geometry based on visual proportions
D_head = 12.5
R_head = D_head / 2.0
H_rim = 0.45
H_dome = 1.75
H_head_total = H_rim + H_dome

R_shank = 1.85
H_shank = 1.25
H_cone = 4.85

L_cross = 7.25
W_cross = 2.15
D_cross = 1.35

# Calculate the center and radius of the sphere for the domed top
# The arc passes through (0, H_head_total) and (R_head, H_rim)
y_c = (H_head_total**2 - R_head**2 - H_rim**2) / (2 * H_dome)
R_sphere = H_head_total - y_c

# Calculate a precise midpoint on the arc to use the threePointArc function
mid_x = R_head / 2.0
mid_y = y_c + math.sqrt(R_sphere**2 - mid_x**2)

# Generate the main solid body by revolving a 2D profile
body = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, H_head_total)
    .threePointArc((mid_x, mid_y), (R_head, H_rim))
    .lineTo(R_head, 0)
    .lineTo(R_shank, 0)
    .lineTo(R_shank, -H_shank)
    .lineTo(0, -H_shank - H_cone)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0), (0, 1))
)

# Create a 2D sketch for the cross recess
cross_sketch = (
    cq.Sketch()
    .rect(L_cross, W_cross)
    .rect(W_cross, L_cross)
)

# Extrude the cross shape to act as a cutting tool
# Start offset above the dome apex to ensure a completely clean top cut
cross_tool = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=H_head_total + 1.0)
    .placeSketch(cross_sketch)
    .extrude(-(D_cross + 1.0))
)

# Apply the cut to form the final drive recess
result = body.cut(cross_tool)

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the screw
head_radius = 4.0
head_rim_height = 0.6
countersink_depth = 2.4
shank_radius = 1.75
shank_length = 19.0
tip_length = 3.5

# Calculate cumulative Z coordinates for the profile
z_rim = -head_rim_height
z_neck = z_rim - countersink_depth
z_shank_end = z_neck - shank_length
z_tip = z_shank_end - tip_length

# Revolved profile points
profile_pts = [
    (0, 0),
    (head_radius, 0),
    (head_radius, z_rim),
    (shank_radius, z_neck),
    (shank_radius, z_shank_end),
    (0, z_tip),
    (0, 0)
]

# Create main screw body
screw = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polyline(profile_pts)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0), (0, 1))
)

# Phillips cross recess parameters
w = 0.5  # Half width of cross arm
l = 2.5  # Half length of cross arm

cross_pts = [
    (w, l), (w, w), (l, w), (l, -w), (w, -w), (w, -l),
    (-w, -l), (-w, -w), (-l, -w), (-l, w), (-w, w), (-w, l)
]

# Create the cross shape extruded downwards
cross_wp = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(cross_pts).close().extrude(-3.0)

# Create a cone to taper the bottom of the cross (mimicking a Phillips bit)
# The cone goes from z=-2.5 (radius 0) up to z=0 (radius 3.2)
cone_wp = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, 0, -2.5)).cone(0, 3.2, 2.5)

# Intersect the extruded cross with the cone to form the final recess tool
recess_wp = cross_wp.intersect(cone_wp)

# Cut the recess from the screw head
result = screw.cut(recess_wp)

import cadquery as cq

# Inferred dimensions based on visual proportions
R_HEAD = 4.4
H_HEAD = 2.75
R_FILLET = 1.6

R_SHAFT = 1.75
L_SHAFT = 11.5
L_TIP = 3.6

W_SLOT = 1.45
L_SLOT = 6.6
D_SLOT = 1.65

# 1. Base Head (Cylinder with a filleted top edge to simulate pan head)
head = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .circle(R_HEAD)
    .extrude(H_HEAD)
    .edges(">Z")
    .fillet(R_FILLET)
)

# 2. Cylindrical Shaft
shaft = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .circle(R_SHAFT)
    .extrude(-L_SHAFT)
)

# 3. Conical Tip
tip_solid = cq.Solid.makeCone(
    radius1=0.0, 
    radius2=R_SHAFT, 
    height=L_TIP, 
    pnt=cq.Vector(0, 0, -L_SHAFT - L_TIP), 
    dir=cq.Vector(0, 0, 1)
)
tip = cq.Workplane("XY").add(tip_solid)

# Combine the main body parts
screw_body = head.union(shaft).union(tip)

# 4. Cross-Slot Cutters (Phillips style V-grooves)
# Create a V-shaped profile on the YZ plane
cutter_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .polyline([
        (-W_SLOT / 2.0, H_HEAD),
        (W_SLOT / 2.0, H_HEAD),
        (0.0, H_HEAD - D_SLOT)
    ])
    .close()
)

# Extrude symmetrically along the X axis to form the first slot
cutter1 = cutter_profile.extrude(L_SLOT / 2.0, both=True)

# Rotate the first cutter 90 degrees around the Z axis to form the perpendicular slot
cutter2 = cutter1.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 90)

# 5. Final Boolean: Cut the slots from the screw body
result = screw_body.cut(cutter1).cut(cutter2)

import cadquery as cq

# Dimensions inferred from the multi-view image
head_R = 4.25
head_H = 2.8
fillet_R = 1.6

step_R = 2.0
step_L = 0.6

cone_L = 7.0

# Cutter dimensions for Phillips drive
cutter_depth = 2.5
w1 = 1.8
l1 = 5.6
shrink = 0.8
w2 = w1 - 2 * shrink
l2 = l1 - 2 * shrink

def get_cross_pts(w, l):
    """Generates counter-clockwise point coordinates for a cross polygon."""
    return [
        (w/2, l/2), (-w/2, l/2), (-w/2, w/2), (-l/2, w/2),
        (-l/2, -w/2), (-w/2, -w/2), (-w/2, -l/2), (w/2, -l/2),
        (w/2, -w/2), (l/2, -w/2), (l/2, w/2), (w/2, w/2)
    ]

# Generate top and bottom profile coordinates for the tapered cut
cross_pts_1 = get_cross_pts(w1, l1)
cross_pts_2 = get_cross_pts(w2, l2)

# 1. Base Head: Cylinder with a top fillet
head = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(head_R).extrude(head_H)
head = head.edges(">Z").fillet(fillet_R)

# 2. Cutter Loft: Tapered cross shape for the drive recess
cutter = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .polyline(cross_pts_1).close()
          .workplane(offset=-cutter_depth)
          .polyline(cross_pts_2).close()
          .loft()
          .translate((0, 0, head_H + 0.1)))

# Cut the Phillips drive into the head
head = head.cut(cutter)

# 3. Cylindrical Step under the head
step = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-step_L).circle(step_R).extrude(step_L)

# 4. Conical Body (Shank tapering to a point)
cone = (cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-step_L).circle(step_R)
        .workplane(offset=-cone_L).circle(0.01) # 0.01 used to prevent zero-radius topology errors
        .loft())

# Final Assembly
result = head.union(step).union(cone)

import cadquery as cq

# Base dimensions inferred from the multi-view image
D_head = 10.5
T_head = 1.3
D_shaft = 4.2
L_shaft = 5.0
L_tip = 5.5

# Cross recess dimensions
W_cross = 6.5
T_cross = 1.6
D_cross = 0.9

# Fillet radius for the transition between shaft and head
fillet_radius = 0.5

# Calculate Z levels for a bottom-up construction along the Z-axis
z_tip = 0.0
z_shaft_start = L_tip
z_head_bottom = L_tip + L_shaft
z_head_top = L_tip + L_shaft + T_head

# Profile points for revolution (drawn on XZ plane)
# The coordinates are (X, Z) where X is the radius
pts = [
    (0, z_tip),                            # Center point at the tip
    (D_shaft / 2.0, z_shaft_start),        # Transition from tip cone to straight shaft
    (D_shaft / 2.0, z_head_bottom),        # Transition from straight shaft to head underside
    (D_head / 2.0, z_head_bottom),         # Outer edge of the head underside
    (D_head / 2.0, z_head_top),            # Outer edge of the head top
    (0, z_head_top)                        # Center point on the head top
]

# Generate main solid body by revolving the profile 360 degrees
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polyline(pts)
    .close()
    .revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
)

# Apply a fillet to the internal corner where the shaft meets the head
result = result.edges(cq.NearestToPointSelector((D_shaft / 2.0, 0, z_head_bottom))).fillet(fillet_radius)

# Create the cross-shaped recess on the top face
result = (
    result.faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .sketch()
    .rect(W_cross, T_cross)
    .rect(T_cross, W_cross)
    .clean() # Merge the two rectangles into a single cross shape
    .finalize()
    .cutBlind(D_cross) # Extrude cut inwards
)
